candidates.notes: H_ENC confirmed: encoder_norm AUROC=0.757 at GPT-2 L6 replicates iter_002 finding (D1: AUROC=0.757). DeLong test confirms encoder_norm significantly outperforms EDA (p=0.0012). At L10, encoder_norm AUROC=0.645 — lower than L6 but still above EDA (0.344 at L10, note: L10 labels use proxy method). Spearman r=0.712 between encoder_norm and EDA indicates partial overlap but they are not redundant. Key finding: absorbed features have mean encoder_norm=3.26 vs. overall mean=2.58 (Cohen's d significant). NOTE: L10 labels are from probe_decoder_alignment proxy (not FeatureAbsorptionCalculator), so L10 AUROC is less reliable as a comparative measure. The correct next step is to generate proper IG labels for L10 using sae_spelling. All Precision@k are zero due to extreme class imbalance (18 positives among 24576); AUPRC is a better practical metric.

go_no_go_gate_assessment.action: Proceed to A2 (theoretical analysis), A3 (cross-arch), B2 (ARS_v2 validation). H_ENC is the primary driving hypothesis for the paper.

C1_omp_pilot_assessment.gate: OMP reconstruction error < feedforward AND absorption direction reported on 3 letters
C1_omp_pilot_assessment.proceed_to_C2_reason: Absorption measurement is at ceiling (98-100% across all conditions). The proxy metric (checking if any absorbed feature fires at all) is too coarse to distinguish feedforward vs OMP effects. The Chanin IG pipeline is required for meaningful comparison. Additionally, 2-pass encoding had b_dec double-subtraction bug causing invalid results.
C1_omp_pilot_assessment.H_OMP_assessment: INCONCLUSIVE — not falsified, but not confirmed. The absorption proxy shows near-100% absorption regardless of encoding method. This is consistent with sparsity landscape dominance, but requires proper IG-based measurement to confirm.
C1_omp_pilot_assessment.key_finding: OMP implementation is valid (recon check passes). The amortization gap experiment requires the full Chanin IG pipeline to generate meaningful absorption rates. Skip C2 and flag for future iteration.

D1_crosshier_probe_training_assessment.gate: Entity-type LR probe trained at all tested layers; at least one layer achieves > 75% accuracy; shuffle gate check completed
D1_crosshier_probe_training_assessment.key_finding: Entity-type probe (ANIMAL vs. inanimate) achieves 100% accuracy at all tested layers. Shuffle gate passes at all layers (shuffled F1=0.48-0.50, well below threshold of 0.60), confirming probes are not degenerate. Negative control (weather vs. time) also shows high accuracy (98-100%), which is expected — the key test in D2 will be whether absorption rates differ between these two hierarchy types. Both probe directions saved for D2 use at all layers. Proceed to D2.
D1_crosshier_probe_training_assessment.note_on_100pct_accuracy: 100% accuracy in pilot is expected and non-alarming — the ANIMAL vs. inanimate distinction is highly separable in GPT-2 residual stream. The key D2 test is whether the SAE features corresponding to animal concepts show higher absorption than those for weather/time distinctions.
